frameworks.name: functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Brain-wide network organization probed via fMRI correlations across states.

frameworks.name: intrinsic vs state-specific network organization
frameworks.one_line: Stable intrinsic architecture coexists with transient state-dependent FC patterns.

frameworks.name: predictive multivariate modeling (PrimeNet)
frameworks.one_line: Multilevel neural-effects model classifies state from FC patterns.

core_question: To what extent does brain-wide functional connectivity differ between naturalistic movie-watching and rest across development?
key_assumptions: Cognitive states share an intrinsic FC organization stable within individual.
State-specific transient FC variations are detectable in fMRI.
Movie-watching is a developmentally appropriate alternative to rest.
manipulations: Age across childhood and young adulthood (6-20).
Movie identity (multiple movies tested for generalization).
key_findings: A widespread FC pattern reliably predicts movie-watching vs rest.
PrimeNet classification generalizes across different movies and ages.
FC patterns in the most predictive networks vary as a function of development.
interpretations: Distinct state-specific FC signatures coexist with intrinsic organization.
Naturalistic vs rest FC differences are developmentally modulated.
